model_narrative: We design a reverse-logistics network that collects used products from primary customers, recovers what it can, and redistributes recovered goods to a second market while disposing of the rest. We decide which collection/inspection centers, recovery centers, and redistribution centers to open, and we decide how much product to ship along every link of the network: from customers to collection/inspection centers, on to recovery centers, then to redistribution centers, out to second-market customers, and from collection/inspection centers to disposal centers for the scrapped portion. We also track any second-market demand we leave unmet. The objective is to minimize the total of fixed opening costs, per-unit shipping costs along all links, and penalty costs for unsatisfied demand.

sets.name: K
sets.members: the primary customer zones that surrender used products, indexed 0..len(K)-1
sets.doc: the set of primary customer zones; each zone returns a known quantity of used products that must all be collected

sets.name: I
sets.members: the candidate collection/inspection center locations, indexed 0..len(I)-1
sets.doc: the set of candidate sites where a collection and inspection center may be opened; returned products arrive here, are inspected, then split into a recoverable stream and a scrapped stream

sets.name: J
sets.members: the candidate recovery center locations, indexed 0..len(J)-1
sets.doc: the set of candidate sites where a recovery (remanufacturing) center may be opened; recoverable products are processed here

sets.name: M
sets.members: the candidate redistribution center locations, indexed 0..len(M)-1
sets.doc: the set of candidate sites where a redistribution center may be opened; recovered products pass through here on the way back to customers

sets.name: L
sets.members: the secondary-market customer zones, indexed 0..len(L)-1
sets.doc: the set of second-market customer zones that demand recovered products

sets.name: N
sets.members: the disposal center locations, indexed 0..len(N)-1
sets.doc: the set of disposal centers that receive scrapped products

params.name: d
params.doc: the demand for recovered products of each second-market customer zone, in product units

params.name: r
params.doc: the quantity of used products that each primary customer zone returns and that must be fully collected, in product units

params.name: s
params.doc: the average disposal fraction; a single number between 0 and 1 giving the share of collected returns that is scrapped rather than recovered, so the recoverable share is one minus this value

params.name: cc
params.doc: the throughput capacity for handling returned products at each candidate collection/inspection center, in product units

params.name: ce
params.doc: the throughput capacity for handling recovered products at each candidate redistribution center, in product units

params.name: cr
params.doc: the throughput capacity for handling recoverable products at each candidate recovery center, in product units

params.name: cd
params.doc: the throughput capacity for handling scrapped products at each disposal center, in product units

params.name: f
params.doc: the fixed cost of opening a collection/inspection center at each candidate site

params.name: g
params.doc: the fixed cost of opening a recovery center at each candidate site

params.name: h
params.doc: the fixed cost of opening a redistribution center at each candidate site

params.name: c
params.doc: the per-unit shipping cost of returned products from a customer zone to a collection/inspection center

params.name: a
params.doc: the per-unit shipping cost of recoverable products from a collection/inspection center to a recovery center

params.name: b
params.doc: the per-unit shipping cost of recovered products from a recovery center to a redistribution center

params.name: e
params.doc: the per-unit shipping cost of recovered products from a redistribution center to a second-market customer zone

params.name: v
params.doc: the per-unit shipping cost of scrapped products from a collection/inspection center to a disposal center

params.name: pi
params.doc: the per-unit penalty cost for demand of a second-market customer that is left unsatisfied

vars.name: X
vars.doc: the quantity of returned products shipped from a customer zone to a collection/inspection center

vars.name: U
vars.doc: the quantity of recoverable products shipped from a collection/inspection center to a recovery center

vars.name: P
vars.doc: the quantity of recovered products shipped from a recovery center to a redistribution center

vars.name: Q
vars.doc: the quantity of recovered products shipped from a redistribution center to a second-market customer zone

vars.name: T
vars.doc: the quantity of scrapped products shipped from a collection/inspection center to a disposal center

vars.name: delta
vars.doc: the quantity of unsatisfied demand at each second-market customer zone

vars.name: Y
vars.doc: equals 1 if a collection/inspection center is opened at the site and 0 otherwise

vars.name: Z
vars.doc: equals 1 if a recovery center is opened at the site and 0 otherwise

vars.name: W
vars.doc: equals 1 if a redistribution center is opened at the site and 0 otherwise

constraints.name: ce)
constraints.intent: Each second-market customer zone must have its demand for recovered products fully accounted for. For every such zone, the total amount of recovered product delivered into it from all redistribution centers, together with the amount of its demand recorded as unmet, must be at least its demand. This lets deliveries to a zone exceed its demand while keeping the recorded shortfall from going negative.

constraints.name: cc)
constraints.intent: Every primary customer zone must surrender all of its returned products. For each such zone, the total quantity of returns it ships out across all collection and inspection centers must exactly equal the amount it returns.

constraints.name: cr)
constraints.intent: At each collection and inspection center the recoverable portion of what arrives must flow on without accumulation. For every such center, the total amount of recoverable product it ships out to recovery centers must equal the recoverable share of all the returns it receives from customer zones, where the recoverable share is what remains after the disposal fraction is removed.

constraints.name: cd)
constraints.intent: At each collection and inspection center the scrapped portion of what arrives must flow on to disposal without accumulation. For every such center, the total amount of scrapped product it ships out to disposal centers must equal the disposal share of all the returns it receives from customer zones.

constraints.name: cr)
constraints.intent: Each redistribution center must pass through exactly what it receives. For every such center, the total amount of recovered product arriving from all recovery centers must equal the total amount it sends out to all second-market customer zones.

constraints.name: ce)
constraints.intent: Each recovery center cannot send out more than it takes in. For every such center, the total amount of recovered product it ships to redistribution centers must not exceed the total amount of recoverable product it receives from collection and inspection centers.

constraints.name: r)
constraints.intent: A collection and inspection center can only handle returns if it is opened, and never beyond its capacity. For every candidate site, the total returns it receives from all customer zones must not exceed its handling capacity when it is opened, and must be zero when it is not opened.

constraints.name: cc)
constraints.intent: A recovery center can only handle recoverable products if it is opened, and never beyond its capacity. For every candidate site, the total recoverable product it receives from all collection and inspection centers must not exceed its handling capacity when it is opened, and must be zero when it is not opened.

constraints.name: cr)
constraints.intent: A redistribution center can only handle recovered products if it is opened, and never beyond its capacity. For every candidate site, the total recovered product it receives from all recovery centers must not exceed its handling capacity when it is opened, and must be zero when it is not opened.

constraints.name: cc)
constraints.intent: No disposal center may receive more scrapped product than it can handle. For every disposal center, the total scrapped product arriving from all collection and inspection centers must not exceed its handling capacity.

query: The city is starting a multi-year road project that will cut off truck access from redistribution center 4 to customers 5, 6 and 7. If center 4 can no longer ship to any of those three, what does the optimizer come back with for cost?
expected_pyomo: model.road1 = Constraint(expr=sum(model.Q[4, l] for l in [5, 6, 7]) <= 0)

query: Suppose the road work expands and ends up blocking center 4 from reaching the whole stretch of customers 2 through 9. With that much of its delivery range gone, what cost does the plan settle at?
expected_pyomo: model.road2 = Constraint(expr=sum(model.Q[4, l] for l in range(2, 10)) <= 0)

query: There's a proposal on the table to consolidate the redistribution tier — keep no more than two redistribution hubs running across the whole network instead of the current spread. If we hold the number of open redistribution centers to at most two, what does the optimizer return for total cost?
expected_pyomo: model.consol_w = Constraint(expr=sum(model.W[m] for m in range(5)) <= 2)

query: Operations wants to avoid leaning too hard on the redistribution hub at location 4. If we hold its total outbound shipments to no more than 1000 units, what does cost come out to?
expected_pyomo: model.cap_w4 = Constraint(expr=sum(model.Q[4, l] for l in range(10)) <= 1000)

model_narrative: We design a reverse-logistics network that collects used products from primary customers, recovers what it can, and redistributes recovered goods to a second market while disposing of the rest. We decide which collection/inspection centers, recovery centers, and redistribution centers to open, and we decide how much product to ship along every link of the network: from customers to collection/inspection centers, on to recovery centers, then to redistribution centers, out to second-market customers, and from collection/inspection centers to disposal centers for the scrapped portion. We also track any second-market demand we leave unmet. The objective is to minimize the total of fixed opening costs, per-unit shipping costs along all links, and penalty costs for unsatisfied demand.

sets.name: K
sets.members: the primary customer zones that surrender used products, indexed 0..len(K)-1
sets.doc: the set of primary customer zones; each zone returns a known quantity of used products that must all be collected

sets.name: I
sets.members: the candidate collection/inspection center locations, indexed 0..len(I)-1
sets.doc: the set of candidate sites where a collection and inspection center may be opened; returned products arrive here, are inspected, then split into a recoverable stream and a scrapped stream

sets.name: J
sets.members: the candidate recovery center locations, indexed 0..len(J)-1
sets.doc: the set of candidate sites where a recovery (remanufacturing) center may be opened; recoverable products are processed here

sets.name: M
sets.members: the candidate redistribution center locations, indexed 0..len(M)-1
sets.doc: the set of candidate sites where a redistribution center may be opened; recovered products pass through here on the way back to customers

sets.name: L
sets.members: the secondary-market customer zones, indexed 0..len(L)-1
sets.doc: the set of second-market customer zones that demand recovered products

sets.name: N
sets.members: the disposal center locations, indexed 0..len(N)-1
sets.doc: the set of disposal centers that receive scrapped products

params.name: d
params.doc: the demand for recovered products of each second-market customer zone, in product units

params.name: r
params.doc: the quantity of used products that each primary customer zone returns and that must be fully collected, in product units

params.name: s
params.doc: the average disposal fraction; a single number between 0 and 1 giving the share of collected returns that is scrapped rather than recovered, so the recoverable share is one minus this value

params.name: cc
params.doc: the throughput capacity for handling returned products at each candidate collection/inspection center, in product units

params.name: ce
params.doc: the throughput capacity for handling recovered products at each candidate redistribution center, in product units

params.name: cr
params.doc: the throughput capacity for handling recoverable products at each candidate recovery center, in product units

params.name: cd
params.doc: the throughput capacity for handling scrapped products at each disposal center, in product units

params.name: f
params.doc: the fixed cost of opening a collection/inspection center at each candidate site

params.name: g
params.doc: the fixed cost of opening a recovery center at each candidate site

params.name: h
params.doc: the fixed cost of opening a redistribution center at each candidate site

params.name: c
params.doc: the per-unit shipping cost of returned products from a customer zone to a collection/inspection center

params.name: a
params.doc: the per-unit shipping cost of recoverable products from a collection/inspection center to a recovery center

params.name: b
params.doc: the per-unit shipping cost of recovered products from a recovery center to a redistribution center

params.name: e
params.doc: the per-unit shipping cost of recovered products from a redistribution center to a second-market customer zone

params.name: v
params.doc: the per-unit shipping cost of scrapped products from a collection/inspection center to a disposal center

params.name: pi
params.doc: the per-unit penalty cost for demand of a second-market customer that is left unsatisfied

vars.name: X
vars.doc: the quantity of returned products shipped from a customer zone to a collection/inspection center

vars.name: U
vars.doc: the quantity of recoverable products shipped from a collection/inspection center to a recovery center

vars.name: P
vars.doc: the quantity of recovered products shipped from a recovery center to a redistribution center

vars.name: Q
vars.doc: the quantity of recovered products shipped from a redistribution center to a second-market customer zone

vars.name: T
vars.doc: the quantity of scrapped products shipped from a collection/inspection center to a disposal center

vars.name: delta
vars.doc: the quantity of unsatisfied demand at each second-market customer zone

vars.name: Y
vars.doc: equals 1 if a collection/inspection center is opened at the site and 0 otherwise

vars.name: Z
vars.doc: equals 1 if a recovery center is opened at the site and 0 otherwise

vars.name: W
vars.doc: equals 1 if a redistribution center is opened at the site and 0 otherwise

constraints.name: ce)
constraints.intent: Each second-market customer zone must have its demand for recovered products fully accounted for. For every such zone, the total amount of recovered product delivered into it from all redistribution centers, together with the amount of its demand recorded as unmet, must be at least its demand. This lets deliveries to a zone exceed its demand while keeping the recorded shortfall from going negative.

constraints.name: cc)
constraints.intent: Every primary customer zone must surrender all of its returned products. For each such zone, the total quantity of returns it ships out across all collection and inspection centers must exactly equal the amount it returns.

constraints.name: cr)
constraints.intent: At each collection and inspection center the recoverable portion of what arrives must flow on without accumulation. For every such center, the total amount of recoverable product it ships out to recovery centers must equal the recoverable share of all the returns it receives from customer zones, where the recoverable share is what remains after the disposal fraction is removed.

constraints.name: cd)
constraints.intent: At each collection and inspection center the scrapped portion of what arrives must flow on to disposal without accumulation. For every such center, the total amount of scrapped product it ships out to disposal centers must equal the disposal share of all the returns it receives from customer zones.

constraints.name: cr)
constraints.intent: Each redistribution center must pass through exactly what it receives. For every such center, the total amount of recovered product arriving from all recovery centers must equal the total amount it sends out to all second-market customer zones.

constraints.name: ce)
constraints.intent: Each recovery center cannot send out more than it takes in. For every such center, the total amount of recovered product it ships to redistribution centers must not exceed the total amount of recoverable product it receives from collection and inspection centers.

constraints.name: r)
constraints.intent: A collection and inspection center can only handle returns if it is opened, and never beyond its capacity. For every candidate site, the total returns it receives from all customer zones must not exceed its handling capacity when it is opened, and must be zero when it is not opened.

constraints.name: cc)
constraints.intent: A recovery center can only handle recoverable products if it is opened, and never beyond its capacity. For every candidate site, the total recoverable product it receives from all collection and inspection centers must not exceed its handling capacity when it is opened, and must be zero when it is not opened.

constraints.name: cr)
constraints.intent: A redistribution center can only handle recovered products if it is opened, and never beyond its capacity. For every candidate site, the total recovered product it receives from all recovery centers must not exceed its handling capacity when it is opened, and must be zero when it is not opened.

constraints.name: cc)
constraints.intent: No disposal center may receive more scrapped product than it can handle. For every disposal center, the total scrapped product arriving from all collection and inspection centers must not exceed its handling capacity.

query: The plan is funneling huge volumes through one or two redistribution hubs while the others barely move product, and our service teams can't staff a hub for that kind of peak load. Shouldn't every redistribution center be held to the same ceiling on outbound deliveries to customers — say no single one moving more than 1300 units out to the second-market zones? What's keeping the model from balancing the load that way?

query: Collection center 2 is carrying noticeably more intake than collection center 4, and the crews at site 4 keep flagging that they're under-utilized while site 2 runs hot. Couldn't we keep the returns routed into center 4 from running below what we put through center 2 — hold center 4's intake to at least ninety percent of center 2's? What's the model seeing that pulls it away from sharing the load like that?
expected_pyomo: model.bal_x4 = Constraint(expr=sum(model.X[k, 4] for k in range(10)) <= 0.9 * sum(model.X[k, 2] for k in range(10)))

query: The plan only commits to three of our five collection/inspection sites, and leaning on so few intake points worries me — one disruption and we lose a big chunk of returns capacity. Wouldn't it be more resilient to commit to at least four of the five collection/inspection centers? What's the tradeoff that's keeping the model from opening that extra site?
expected_pyomo: model.commit_y = Constraint(expr=sum(model.Y[i] for i in range(5)) >= 4)

For every candidate site, the total returns it receives from all customer zones must not exceed its handling capacity when it is opened, and must be zero when it is not opened.

constraints.name: cc)
constraints.intent: A recovery center can only handle recoverable products if it is opened, and never beyond its capacity. For every candidate site, the total recoverable product it receives from all collection and inspection centers must not exceed its handling capacity when it is opened, and must be zero when it is not opened.

constraints.name: cr)
constraints.intent: A redistribution center can only handle recovered products if it is opened, and never beyond its capacity. For every candidate site, the total recovered product it receives from all recovery centers must not exceed its handling capacity when it is opened, and must be zero when it is not opened.

constraints.name: cc)
constraints.intent: No disposal center may receive more scrapped product than it can handle. For every disposal center, the total scrapped product arriving from all collection and inspection centers must not exceed its handling capacity.

query: Two things about this plan bother me as a package: it puts all its redistribution weight on hub 4, and it pushes an enormous throughput through recovery center 2 — both look like single points of failure. Couldn't we take hub 4 out of the redistribution picture entirely and at the same time hold recovery center 2's total outflow under 2000 units so it isn't carrying the whole network? What's the model seeing that makes it resist de-risking on both fronts at once?
expected_pyomo:
model.drop_w4 = Constraint(expr=model.W[4] <= 0)
model.cap_p2 = Constraint(expr=sum(model.P[2, m] for m in range(5)) <= 2000)
